'''Paul Rowley''' (born 12 March 1975) is an English professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and former professional rugby league footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. 

An [[England national rugby league team|England]] international {{rlp|hk}}, he played at club level for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/paul-rowley/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref> After retiring as a player, Rowley began a coaching career and was appointed head coach of [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in 2012, leading the club to [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] titles in [[2014 RFL Championship|2014]] and [[2015 RFL Championship|2015]] before departing at the beginning of the 2016 season. He subsequently was the head coach of the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Coach statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Rowley was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester]], England.

==Playing career==
Having begun his career at his hometown club, Leigh, Rowley appeared in the [[Super League]] for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]], returning to his hometown club in 2002. He retired as a player in April 2007 after his 400th career appearance,<ref>{{cite web |title=Rowley calls it a day |url=https://www.theboltonnews.co.uk/news/1326186.rowley-calls-it-a-day/ |website=The Bolton News |access-date=12 June 2022 |date=12 April 2007}}</ref> and joined the coaching staff at Leigh Centurions.

===International honours===
Rowley won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Halifax in 1996 against France (sub), and in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] against Australia, Russia, and Ireland.

==Coaching career==
===Leigh Centurions===
Rowley was appointed first team coach of Leigh Centurions on a permanent basis in January 2012. After a successful first season in charge which included guiding the club to 2nd in the Championship and a Northern Rail Cup semi-final, Rowley was named Championship Coach of the year. In February 2013 Rowley signed a new contract to keep him at the club until the end of 2015. Having won back-to-back Championship titles with Leigh in 2014 and 2015, Rowley resigned as Leigh Centurions head coach 10 days before the start of the 2016 Championship season, citing personal reasons.<ref name="Paul Rowley: Leigh Centurions head coach resigns">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35429726|title=Paul Rowley: Leigh Centurions head coach resigns|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=28 January 2016|accessdate=28 January 2016}}</ref>
===Toronto Wolfpack===
On 27 April 2016 he was confirmed as the inaugural head coach of the [[Toronto Wolfpack]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Toronto Wolfpack to join third tier of English rugby league in 2017 |date=2016-04-27 |website=[[The Globe and Mail]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201111202306/https://www.theglobeandmail.com/sports/more-sports/toronto-wolfpack-to-join-third-tier-of-english-rugby-league-in-2017/article29770185/ |archive-date=2020-11-11 |url-status=live |url=https://www.theglobeandmail.com/sports/more-sports/toronto-wolfpack-to-join-third-tier-of-english-rugby-league-in-2017/article29770185/}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 5 November 2021, Rowley was announced as the new head-coach of the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Nov 2021|accessdate=6 Nov 2021|title=Paul Rowley confirmed as new Salford coach|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/paul-rowley-new-salford-coach/}}</ref>
In Rowley's first season in charge of Salford, the club started poorly and at one stage were sitting second last on the table before they went on to win eleven games which saw them qualify for the playoffs. After upsetting Huddersfield 28–0 in week one of the playoffs, the club played against St Helens in the semi-final in which they lost 19–12. In the 2023 Super League season, Rowley guided Salford to a 7th-placed finish as the club missed the playoffs by two points. In the 2024 Super League season, Rowley took Salford to the playoffs again but they were eliminated in the first week by Leigh. In 2025, Salford were hit with major financial issues and a limited playing squad. Under Rowley, the club would finish bottom of the Super League recording just three wins for the entire season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62939943|title=Super League semi-final: St Helens 19–12 Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport |access-date=7 October 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.prolificnorth.co.uk/news/struggling-salford-red-devils-hit-with-two-point-deduction-while-club-addresses-weekend-fan-protests/|title=Salford Red Devils dealt double blow as nightmare 2025 takes another turn|website=www.prolificnorth.co.uk}}</ref>
===St Helens===
In November 2025, Rowley was appointed as the new head coach of St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cpqypq48yvgo/|title=Rowley 'not pressured for positive Saints start'|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In Rowley's first game in charge of St Helens, the club would win 98-2 against [[Workington Town]] in the third round of the Challenge Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/25834740.paul-rowley-said-st-helens-cup-win-workington/|title=What Paul Rowley said about St Helens' cup win at Workington|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Paul Rowley is the son of the rugby league {{rlp|pr}} who played in the 1970s and 1980s for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], [[Workington Town]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Carlisle RLFC|Carlisle]]; Allan Rowley (born {{birth year and age|1951}}). Paul Rowley has two sons, Alex and Oliver.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}
